fields.description:
## Context

When /dso:sprint begins executing an epic, Phase 1's Preplanning Gate (Step 2a) checks whether children tasks are ambiguous but never validates that children collectively cover all of the epic's success criteria. A child set can be concrete and well-defined yet still leave one or more SCs completely unaddressed. These gaps go undetected until the completion verifier runs at sprint close — wasting multiple sub-agent execution cycles on an epic that was never fully specified against its own acceptance criteria.

## Success Criteria

- Sprint Phase 1 (Preplanning Gate Step 2a) runs a 3-tier SC coverage check — haiku first pass, sonnet targeted pass, opus final arbiter — before Phase 3 batch preparation, for epic-typed tickets with existing children
- Haiku confirms only SCs it can validate by citing a specific covering child; any SC it cannot cite a covering child for is escalated to sonnet
- Sonnet evaluates each escalated SC independently (without knowledge of why it was escalated) and returns COVERED, MISSING, or UNSURE per SC; UNSURE SCs are passed to opus
- Opus is the final arbiter for UNSURE SCs, returning COVERED or MISSING; opus defaults to COVERED when coverage intent is plausible but wording is ambiguous
- When any SC is determined MISSING post-cascade, sprint writes a REPLAN_TRIGGER: sc_coverage comment on the epic listing the missing SCs, then routes to /dso:preplanning (at least one child has type:story) or /dso:implementation-plan (all children have type:task)
- When all SCs are COVERED post-cascade, sprint proceeds normally to Phase 3 without invoking preplanning
- Tests cover each tier's routing logic — including a test where an epic with 5 SCs and children covering only 3 triggers gap detection and routes to the correct decomposition skill based on children type
- Dedicated prompt files exist for each tier (haiku, sonnet, opus) defining input schema (SC list + children descriptions) and output schema (per-SC verdict + escalation list), referenced from the sprint SKILL.md step

## Scenario Analysis

- Haiku tier silent truncation on large SC lists (high, runtime): When an epic has 10+ SCs, haiku may silently truncate the list, returning COVERED only for processed SCs. Unreviewed SCs are treated as covered.
- Tier output parse failure causes gate to silently pass (critical, runtime): Malformed JSON from any tier agent causes the error handler to treat it as "no MISSING findings," passing the gate silently.
- New gate step breaks sprint SKILL.md step numbering (high, deployment): Inserting a new step shifts step numbers. Existing CHECKPOINT ticket comments referencing old step numbers cause misaligned resume behavior.
- Haiku model ID unavailable in deployment environment (high, deployment): Hard-coded haiku model ID fails in environments where the alias differs, blocking sprint entry for all epics.
- sc_coverage.enabled absent from dso-config.conf silently disables gate (high, configuration): Missing config key defaults to falsy, silently disabling SC coverage validation on upgrade.
- Empty epic SC list vacuously passes (medium, configuration): Epics with 0 SCs pass all tiers trivially, masking a structural data quality gap.
- Single-word SC causes unresolvable UNSURE loop (high, configuration): Vague SCs cause all tiers to return UNSURE/MISSING, triggering repeated replans bounded only by max_replan_cycles.

## Prompt Alignment Findings

Behavioral soundness: MEDIUM. Three risks for implementation to address:
- Haiku confidence over-reporting: Replace open-ended confidence instruction with a citation requirement — haiku must name the specific child that covers the SC. No citation means mandatory escalation.
- Sonnet anchoring: Strip escalation provenance from sonnet's input. Present SCs as a neutral evaluation list without mentioning haiku's uncertainty.
- Opus MISSING-bias: Add explicit tie-breaking rule — return COVERED when intent is plausible but wording is ambiguous. MISSING is for demonstrably absent coverage only.

## Dependencies

None. Epic dso-94ya's preflight check validates project state (CI/validation health) and is a distinct gate — no overlap or sequencing dependency.

## Approach

Insert the 3-tier SC coverage check (haiku → sonnet → opus) as a new named step in the Preplanning Gate (after Step 2a ambiguity check, before Phase 3). Dedicated agent prompt files define per-tier input/output contracts. Sprint parses tier output and routes via the REPLAN_TRIGGER comment pattern using the new sc_coverage trigger type — distinct from the existing drift/failure/validation/review types — when SCs are MISSING.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered — no bright-line conditions fired
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered — 7 scenarios surviving blue team filter
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
- Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0): not triggered
- Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5): not triggered
- LLM-instruction signal (Step 5): triggered — keyword: skill file modifications
